cable_setup.mig_gun: Connected to Wire Feed mechanism via Cable Connector

MIG.gas_type: C25 (75% Argon / 25% CO2) for steel, Stainless Tri-Mix for stainless steel, 100% Argon for aluminum (spool gun)
MIG.gun_angle: Push angle: 0-15° away from weld direction
MIG.ctwd: 1/2" or less (Contact Tip to Work Distance)
steps: Turn Power Switch OFF and unplug Welder
Open door via Door Latch
Install wire spool (must unwind clockwise)
Check feed roller matches wire size (V-Groove for solid core)
Insert Gun Cable Connector into Wire Feed mechanism — must be FULLY inserted or gas will leak
Connect Wire Feed Control Cable to socket inside machine
Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Negative (-) Socket
Plug Wire Feed Power Cable into Positive (+) Socket
Set up shielding gas: mount cylinder, attach regulator, connect gas hose to welder gas inlet
Cut bent/crimped wire end, feed 12" into Wire Inlet Liner
Set wire in Feed Roller groove, latch Feed Tensioner
Remove Nozzle and Contact Tip from MIG Gun
Lay MIG Gun cable straight, plug in power cord
Press Cold Wire Feed Switch until wire feeds through 2 inches
Test tension: feed wire against wood block at 2-3 inches, wire should bend not stop
Replace Contact Tip (correct size for wire), replace Nozzle
Cut wire to 1/2" stickout
Close door securely
settings_sequence: Turn Main Control Knob to MIG process
Press Main Control Knob to select
Confirm polarity and gas on screen
Turn Left Knob to set wire diameter
Turn Right Knob to set material thickness
Auto weld settings: Left Knob = Wire Feed Speed (Amperage), Right Knob = Voltage

cable_setup.mig_gun: Connected to Wire Feed mechanism via Cable Connector

Flux-Cored.feed_tension: 2-3 (for flux-cored wire — too much force will crush it)
steps: Turn Power Switch OFF and unplug Welder
Open door via Door Latch
Install wire spool (must unwind clockwise)
Check feed roller matches wire size (Knurled Groove for flux-cored)
Insert Gun Cable Connector into Wire Feed mechanism — must be FULLY inserted
Connect Wire Feed Control Cable to socket inside machine
Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Positive (+) Socket — NOTE: opposite of MIG
Plug Wire Feed Power Cable into Negative (-) Socket — NOTE: opposite of MIG
NO gas setup needed
Cut bent/crimped wire end, feed 12" into Wire Inlet Liner
Set wire in Feed Roller groove, latch Feed Tensioner (tension 2-3, lower than solid wire)
Remove Nozzle and Contact Tip from MIG Gun
Lay MIG Gun cable straight, plug in power cord
Press Cold Wire Feed Switch until wire feeds through 2 inches
Test tension: feed wire against wood block at 2-3 inches
Replace Contact Tip (correct size), replace Nozzle
Cut wire to 1/2" stickout
Close door securely
Flux-Cored.key_difference_from_mig: Polarity is REVERSED compared to MIG. Ground goes to Positive, Wire Feed goes to Negative. No gas needed.

TIG.arc_start_method: Lift-style TIG: push Foot Pedal and touch Electrode to workpiece, then lift. Without pedal: touch and lift.
TIG.electrode_tip: Grind to blunt point, conical portion 2.5x electrode diameter. Grinding direction parallel to length.
torch_assembly: Thread Collet Body into front of Torch
Thread Ceramic Nozzle onto Collet Body
Insert Collet into back of Torch
TIG.torch_distance: 1 to 1.5 times the diameter of the Electrode
steps: Turn Power Switch OFF and unplug Welder
Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Positive (+) Socket, twist clockwise to lock
Plug TIG Torch Cable into Negative (-) Socket, twist clockwise to lock
Connect Foot Pedal Cable through front hole to Foot Pedal Socket inside machine (optional)
Set up 100% Argon cylinder: mount, clean valve, attach regulator, connect gas hose from TIG Torch to regulator outlet
Assemble TIG Torch: Collet Body → Ceramic Nozzle → Collet → Tungsten Electrode → Back Cap
Sharpen tungsten electrode to blunt conical point
Plug in power cord (do NOT plug into wall yet)
Plug into wall outlet, turn Power Switch ON
Select TIG process on LCD, set rod diameter and material thickness
Turn Right Knob to ON to energize TIG Torch — WARNING: OCV now present
Open gas cylinder valve all the way, set flow to 10-25 SCFH
Open valve on TIG Torch to start gas flow
Initiate arc: push Foot Pedal + touch electrode to workpiece + lift

welding_technique.filler_addition: Add TIG Rod to front end of weld puddle. Remove rod each time electrode is advanced but keep within gas shield.
welding_technique.finishing: Release Foot Pedal but keep Torch on weld puddle until solidified

steps: Turn Power Switch OFF and unplug Welder
Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Negative (-) Socket, twist clockwise to lock
Plug Electrode Holder Cable into Positive (+) Socket, twist clockwise to lock
Plug power cord into Power Input Socket
Set Electrode Holder on nonconductive, nonflammable surface
Plug into wall outlet, turn Power Switch ON
Select Stick process on LCD
Set electrode type (60xx or 70xx), diameter, and material thickness
Turn Right Knob to ON to energize Electrode Holder — WARNING: OCV now present
Place bare metal end of Stick Electrode into Electrode Holder jaws
Stroke workpiece lightly to ignite arc

welding_technique.arc_distance: Same distance as diameter of bare metal end of electrode
welding_technique.bead_direction: Drag Electrode to back end of weld puddle

polarity_summary.description: Critical: each process requires different cable configurations. Wrong polarity causes porosity, poor penetration, and excessive spatter.

steps: Remove Wingnut and Spacer
Place spool over Spindle against Brake Pad
Spool must unwind CLOCKWISE
Replace Spacer, secure with Wingnut
1_2_lb_spool.warning: If Wire Spool can spin freely, Wingnut is too loose. Wire will unravel and cause tangling.

steps: Remove Wingnut and Spacer
Place Spool Adapter over Spindle against Brake Pad
Place spool over Adapter, align pin with hole
Spool must unwind CLOCKWISE
Replace Spacer, secure with Wingnut
Screw Spool Knob into Adapter
10_12_lb_spool.warning: Same as above — Wingnut must be snug.

change_procedure: Remove knob to expose Feed Roller
Flip or replace roller — confirm correct type and size matches wire
Screw Feed Roller Knob back in
